title: The Rat Race
summary: There are no shortcuts in life. Stop looking for easy ways..
content:
A rat wanted to achieve something big in life. It wanted to live a life which wasn't ordinary, but it knew there isn't an easy way around (I will refer to the rat as he/him). Still he was ready to make sacrifices, and he was ambitious and knew with this mindset he can actually achieve his goal.

One day when the rat was aimlessly walking, it saw a group of rats. These rats had either failed the Rat race themselves and wanted the same fate for the other rats, or wanted lesser rats to compete with. Thus they were discouraging other rats from quitting the race and referred to this competition as something bad for our mental health even though the race was actually making rats mentally strong and increasing their willpower.

Unfortunately many rats were getting trapped in this trap set by these menaces to society. They spread this plague through videos, showing fake wealth, breaking the spirits of the young minds. This has become really common nowadays and I don't know why people aren't really paying attention to this.

There simply is no easy way around, you have to participate in this rat race, and actually win this race. That's what your parents want, that's what society wants from you.

I am not telling you it's about marks or getting good grades, it's just how fun it is. There are such beautiful things to study in science, it's so fun to know about our past, it's so fun to know how things actually work. Studying is fun only when u make it seem. At times it's so fun when you get your result and it's actually great marks, no feeling can beat that.

Like a famous saying goes "The harder you work for something, the greater you'll feel when you achieve it."

Just trust me, nothing in this entire world can beat that feeling and there is always a feeling in the heart of pure joy conveying "I am making my parents proud, I am putting their hard work to good use". Your future self would probably be begging you to stop wasting time, stop finding easy ways around, stop delaying. This is just stealing the life you could have had.

Just imagine looking back one year from now and wondering why didn't u start today. The thing is the best time was when u were born, and the second best time is right now. The biggest mistake is waiting for the right time, waiting till you feel ready.

I am not telling you to start studying 12 hours from today. Action, no matter how small will build momentum. Believe in yourself you can do it, listen to your parents, they know what's better for you, and there is no easy way around.
